title: T-006 — Migration: routings and routing_operations tables
prompt: Create Drizzle migration for §5.6 routing tables. `routings`(id PK, org_id, item_id FK items, version INT default 1, status TEXT CHECK in ('draft','approved','active','superseded'), effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE, approved_by, approved_at, UNIQUE(org_id,item_id,version)). `routing_operations`(id PK, routing_id FK routings ON DELETE CASCADE, op_no INT, op_code TEXT, op_name TEXT, line_id UUID nullable FK production_lines, machine_id UUID nullable FK machines, setup_time_min INT default 0, run_time_per_unit_sec NUMERIC(10,2), cost_per_hour NUMERIC(10,4), manufacturing_operation_name TEXT, UNIQUE(routing_id,op_no)). RLS on routings, cascade-driven for routing_operations. RED command: `pnpm vitest run tests/db/routing.migration.test.ts`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Routings + routing_operations with cascading delete and resource FKs.
pipeline_inputs.details: FK to production_lines and machines comes from 02-SETTINGS — if those tables exist, declare hard FK. If not present in test fixture, declare soft (UUID) and document in ADR-update note. manufacturing_operation_name pairs with bom_lines field of same name for cross-link queries.
acceptance_criteria: Given migration applied, when describing routing_operations, then UNIQUE(routing_id,op_no) is enforced.
Given a routings row deleted, when querying routing_operations, then dependent rows are removed via CASCADE.
Given two versions for same item exist, when UNIQUE(org_id,item_id,version) constraint is hit, then duplicate fails.
test_strategy: RED tests for unique constraints + cascade.
risk_red_lines: Do not pre-seed any routings or operations.
